title: feat: migrate qwen-large and minimax to Fireworks AI
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Lighting some Fireworks
gist.blurb: We swapped the engines under Qwen Large and Minimax over to Fireworks AI. Qwen gets a bump to the massive 396B flagship model, which means smarter text and fewer headaches.
gist.summary: Migrated the qwen-large and minimax text models to use Fireworks AI as their backend provider, upgrading Qwen to the 396B flagship version.
gist.impact: Requests to qwen-large and minimax will now route through Fireworks AI, delivering higher quality responses from the upgraded Qwen model.
gist.visual_concept: Fireworks AI migration. Visuals: literal fireworks, but cozy and safe. The bee mascot and the monitor robot setting off a small, beautiful firework in a digital garden.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and the chunky monitor-head robot setting off a small, sparkling firework in a digital garden. The firework illuminates the scene with warm light. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and the chunky monitor-head robot setting off a small, sparkling firework in a digital garden. The firework illuminates the scene with warm light. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley.
